content: BLOCKING: (1) CalendarServiceException at google_calendar_service.dart:190 interpolates e.message from Google API into thrown exception — leaks server-side error detail to callers/UI. Fix: use fixed template with status code only. (2) confirmCalendarEvent() TOCTOU race — state cleared optimistically before DB insert, allowing double-tap duplicate event creation. Fix: guard with isWaitingForAgent check. ADVISORY: (3) updateStatus/updateSyncStatus accept raw String — add validation or enum type. (4) rawUserMessage synced verbatim to Supabase — privacy risk for sensitive health/legal data. Consider excluding from sync. (5) googleCalendarServiceProvider always returns null — fragile pattern. (6) signIn() swallows all exceptions as null — error indistinguishable from cancellation. (7) Voice confirmation 8s ambient audio window. (8) _cancelPendingEvents fetches all sessions. (9) duration_minutes Infinity edge case in extraction.

FINDINGS: (1-HIGH) googleCalendarServiceProvider is a stub returning null — real service constructed inline in SessionNotifier._createGoogleCalendarEvent, bypassing provider graph. Boundary violation. (2-MED) EventExtractionService header claims ConversationLayer pattern but does not implement it — misleading comment vs ADR-0020 sec4. (3-MED) dismissCalendarEvent/dismissReminder asymmetry — each clears its own field but not the other, creating latent bug surface if both are non-null. Fix: shared _clearPendingIntents helper. (4-MED) _cancelPendingEvents fetches all sessions globally then filters in Dart — add getPendingEventsForSession to DAO. (5-MED) Regex extraction layer lacks range validation that LLM layer applies. (6-LOW) Silent title truncation at 200 chars. (7-LOW) Voice orchestrator calendar callbacks null-guard drops confirmations silently. (8-LOW) Sentinel pattern undocumented. STRENGTHS: ADR-0020 compliance is thorough, sealed ExtractionResult is correct, injectable callable pattern is consistent, _routeByIntent exhaustive switch is sound, sync pattern is consistent.

FINDINGS: (1-HIGH) SessionNotifier calendar methods entirely untested: confirmCalendarEvent, dismissCalendarEvent, _cancelPendingEvents, deferCalendarEvent — these are the primary write paths. (2-HIGH) VoiceSessionOrchestrator.confirmCalendarEvent() entirely untested — voice yes/no, timeout, callback dispatch. (3-MED) uploadSession calendar UPSERT branch untested — client returns null in test fake. (4-MED) Short-message intent classifier edge cases not pinned (4-word boundary with calendar/reminder signals). (5-MED) _nextWeekday same-day behavior not tested (Wednesday asks for on Wednesday). (6-MED) syncPendingCalendarEvents loop not exercised with real events. (7-LOW) _formatTimeForSpeech noon/midnight cases. (8-LOW) _cancelPendingEvents cross-session safety not asserted. STRENGTHS: Injectable callable pattern avoids platform mocking. 24 pinned regression tests. Event extraction 100% coverage. DAO tests properly isolated with in-memory DB. CalendarEventCard tests all 4 states.

FINDINGS: (1-HIGH) syncPendingCalendarEvents per-event serial UPSERTs — 10 events = 10 HTTP round-trips. Batch like message UPSERT. (2-HIGH) _cancelPendingEvents full table scan + per-event updates — replace with single cancelEventsForSession DAO method. (3-MED) Missing composite index on (status, sync_status) for getEventsToSync query. (4-MED) pendingCalendarEventsCountProvider materializes all rows to count — add countAllPendingEvents aggregate method. (5-MED) GoogleConnectionNotifier async constructor may block first paint if eagerly read. (6-LOW) Word-count allocation before signal checks in classifyMulti. (7-LOW) Timer leak in voice confirmation outer timeout. STRENGTHS: Regex patterns static final (compiled once). Extraction sets isExtracting before async call. LazyDatabase correct. countPendingForSession uses selectOnly aggregate.

BLOCKING: (1) Timezone field MISSING in gcal.EventDateTime — events will display at wrong times for ALL non-UTC users. Code passes startTime.toUtc() without timeZone field. Google Calendar renders as UTC. A 3pm meeting shows as 3pm UTC = wrong local time. (2) 5-pending-event cap specified in ADR-0020 sec7 but countPendingForSession() is NEVER called in _handleCalendarIntent or _handleReminderIntent — cap is unimplemented. ADVISORY: (3) Optimistic success message before API completes — user sees Added to calendar before Google API returns, masking failures. (4) _calendarIntentPattern lacks past-tense guard — journaling about scheduling triggers extraction. (5) rawUserMessage privacy surface in Supabase. HIDDEN ASSUMPTIONS: No Google Tasks API consideration for reminders. 8s voice timeout not calibrated. No background sync retry mechanism. STRENGTHS: Injectable callable pattern is genuinely elegant. Sealed ExtractionResult is the right pattern. Dual state machine is sound. Temporal disambiguation is sophisticated.

Phase 11 review complete. 5 specialists: security (0.89), architecture (0.87), QA (0.88), performance (0.87), independent (0.82). Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-CHANGES. 4 blocking findings, 18+ advisory. BLOCKING: (B1) Timezone field missing in EventDateTime — events at wrong times for ALL non-UTC users. Independent-perspective found that gcal.EventDateTime is constructed without timeZone field, causing UTC rendering. (B2) CalendarServiceException leaks e.message from Google API errors — security-specialist found server error detail propagated to callers. (B3) 5-event pending cap (ADR-0020 sec7) not enforced — countPendingForSession() exists but never called in _handleCalendarIntent/_handleReminderIntent. (B4) confirmCalendarEvent TOCTOU race — state cleared optimistically before DB insert allows double-tap duplicate creation. TOP ADVISORY: _cancelPendingEvents should be session-scoped DAO query (architecture+performance). Batch calendar event UPSERTs (performance). Add composite index on (status, sync_status) (performance). SessionNotifier calendar methods untested (QA). Voice orchestrator confirmCalendarEvent untested (QA). rawUserMessage privacy in Supabase sync (security+independent). googleCalendarServiceProvider stub pattern (architecture+security). Dismiss methods field-clearing asymmetry (architecture). Optimistic success message before API completion (independent).
